title: theme — module hooks
description: Cross-aggregate lifecycle callbacks. Each hook is a named class + a single-invoke handler wired into an event/observer via the module's service provider. Distinct from listeners (external event subscribers) — hooks are internal orchestration.

hooks.name: RegenerateCssOnThemeActivated
hooks.action: Dispatch RegenerateRenderedCssJob for the tenant. Runs post-commit so a failed rebuild never rolls back the activation itself.
hooks.notes: The 'CSS is stale until the rebuild lands' window is bounded by the job's execution time (typically < 5s). During that window, downstream consumers read the prior theme_snapshot from cache. This is intentional — availability over consistency.

hooks.name: DeactivatePriorThemeOnActivate
hooks.action: Inside the same DB transaction: find prior active theme in same (tenant_id, mode); set is_active=false + deactivated_at=now(). Enforces the unique_active_theme_per_tenant_per_mode partial index without needing the caller to remember the two-step 'deactivate old, activate new' dance.
hooks.notes: Runs INSIDE the observer (same DB txn), not as an after-commit hook — the invariant would break if the deactivation were reordered.

hooks.name: FreezeTokensSnapshotOnActivate
hooks.action: Resolve the cascade: platform_default_preset ← selected_preset ← theme_token_overrides. Materialise into Theme.tokens_snapshot (jsonb). This snapshot is IMMUTABLE while is_active=true; subsequent preset edits or override changes DO NOT mutate an active theme's snapshot — they land as new sibling rows.
hooks.notes: The load-bearing invariant: mid-session preset edits by an admin never break rendering for logged-in users.

hooks.name: PurgeExpiredThemeSnapshotsOnRetentionCap
hooks.action: Per tenant, per mode: retain the last-N Theme rows (N = 5 default, 20 for theme_rollback_extended tenants); hard-delete older rows. Rollback UI only offers themes that survive this pass.
hooks.notes: Compliance-relevant — the retention policy is auditable via compliance::RetentionRun.
